name: Bard Quartet
description: Quad programmable quantizer with integrated keyboard interface. Four channels, 8 harmonies, 10 memory slots. Supports microtuning, arpeggiator, channel grouping, and harmony progressions for full song structures.

controls.name: Keyboard Buttons
controls.description: 12 keyboard buttons to set notes per scale per channel. Active note shines brighter for real-time note monitoring.
controls.detail: One octave of chromatic keys · C# and D# = Load/Save in Memory menu

controls.name: Harmony Potentiometer
controls.description: Navigates between 8 different harmony locations for the active channel, enabling harmonic progressions. Also used for microtuning (+/-50 cents).
controls.detail: 8 harmony positions per channel · ±50 cents in microtuning mode

controls.name: Channel Buttons
controls.description: Select the active channel being edited (1–4). Hold + another channel button to create a group that shares scale settings.
controls.detail: 4 channel buttons · grouped channels share LED illumination

controls.name: Edit Button
controls.description: Opens Edit menu for Octave setting, Trigger Out assignment, Gate In assignment, and Transpose mode per channel and harmony.
controls.detail: Octave: -3 to +3 · hold 2s for Tuning Mode output

controls.name: µTune / Option Button
controls.description: Opens microtuning menu to detune any note ±50 cents. Also used with Edit to access Options menu. Long press with Harmony pot dissociates pot from current harmony.
controls.detail: ±50 cents per note · Edit+µTune = Options menu

controls.name: ARPG / Memory Button
controls.description: Opens Arpeggiator menu (hold channel button to enable arp per channel). With Edit = Memory menu for saving/loading 10 slots.
controls.detail: Arpeggiator modes: Up / Down / Pendulum / Spiral / Random · 1–4 octave spread

inputs.name: IN 1–4 (Channel CV)
inputs.description: V/Oct CV inputs for each of the four quantizer channels.

inputs.name: TRSP (Transpose CV)
inputs.description: Transpose CV input. Can work as pre-quantize, post-quantize, or octave transposer per channel (set in Edit menu).

inputs.name: GT IN (Gate Input)
inputs.description: Gate input used as Track & Hold (default) or Sample & Hold (rising-edge, selectable in Options). Assigned per channel in Edit menu.

inputs.name: HRMN (Harmony CV)
inputs.description: CV input for Harmony potentiometer position, selecting between the 8 harmony locations.

outputs.name: OUT 1–4 (Channel CV)
outputs.description: Quantized V/Oct outputs for each channel. Supports V/Oct, Hz/V, and 1.2V/Oct output norms.

outputs.name: HRMN (Harmony CV)
outputs.description: Harmony CV output mirroring the current harmony position for use with other modules.

outputs.name: TRIG OUT
outputs.description: Trigger output that fires each time a note change is detected on an assigned channel.

behaviors.name: Scale Programming
behaviors.description: Use keyboard buttons to activate/deactivate notes per channel per harmony. Up to 320 scales storable (4 channels × 8 harmonies × 10 memory slots).

behaviors.name: Harmony Progression
behaviors.description: 8 independent harmony settings per channel. Navigate via pot or CV to create chord progressions and song structures. Changes also affect Edit, Arp, microtuning, and grouping.

behaviors.name: Arpeggiator
behaviors.description: Each channel can become an arpeggiator driven by triggers at its CV input instead of continuous voltage. Modes: Up, Down, Pendulum, Spiral, Random. 1–4 octave spread per channel and harmony.

behaviors.name: Microtuning
behaviors.description: Each note in each channel and harmony can be detuned ±50 cents independently. Tuning setting is stored per harmony. Harmony pot navigates freely when µTune held.

behaviors.name: Channel Grouping
behaviors.description: Grouped channels share scale settings (and optionally Edit, Microtuning, Arpeggiator settings per Options menu). Indicated by simultaneous LED illumination.

behaviors.name: Channel Interaction (Normalization / Shift Register)
behaviors.description: Option per channel (CH 2–4): F# = no interaction, G# = Normalisation (when 0V at IN, channel follows previous channel's input), A# = Shift Register (requires Gate In enabled for that channel; on GT IN rising edge, channel samples previous channel's value). Enables cascading voice effects.

patchIdeas.name: Harmonic progression sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: LFO or sequencer drives HRMN CV to walk 8 chord positions while all 4 channels quantize a shared melody line.
steps: On each channel (press Channel button 1, program scale, then 2, 3, 4): program the 8 harmonies on each channel to form chord voicings (e.g. CH 1 = root, CH 2 = 3rd, CH 3 = 5th, CH 4 = 7th).
Press Edit + µTune to open Options; confirm F# on keyboard is ON for channels you DON'T want interaction (independent quantizing).
Patch a slow sequencer or LFO → HRMN (Harmony CV input, jack 6).
Patch your pitch CV source → IN 1 (jack 3).
Mult the same pitch CV → IN 2, IN 3, IN 4 so all four channels quantize the same note against their respective harmonies.
Patch OUT 1 → VCO 1 V/Oct, OUT 2 → VCO 2, OUT 3 → VCO 3, OUT 4 → VCO 4.
Advance the HRMN sequencer slowly (one step every bar) and watch the Harmony LED ring 1–8 cycle.
Mix the four VCOs to hear chord changes underneath the melody.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — four quantized V/Oct pitch CVs, -3 to +7V, voicing changes with HRMN position.
patchIdeas.hear: Melody-over-chord: one shared note line played on four voices whose individual pitches shift each harmony step, producing a I-vi-IV-V style progression as HRMN walks 1→8.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
- Sequencer (CV) >> Bard Quartet (Harmony CV) // advances 8 chord positions
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Polyphonic arp
patchIdeas.patch: All four channels run as independent arpeggiators clocked at IN 1–4; TRSP transposes the whole quartet.
steps: Press ARPG (button F) to enter the Arpeggiator menu — ARPG LED lights solid.
Hold Channel button 1 and press ARPG to turn CH 1 into an arpeggiator (channel LED blinks). Repeat for channels 2, 3, 4.
In the Arp menu, use the keyboard column to pick Mode (Up ▲ / Down ▼ / Pendulum / Spiral / Random) and octave spread 1–4 per channel/harmony.
Press ARPG again to exit the menu.
Program the scale on each channel — these become the notes the arp walks.
Patch a clock → IN 1 (jack 3). Mult the same clock to IN 2, IN 3, IN 4 (arp clocks replace CV).
Patch an LFO or keyboard → TRSP (jack 4) and set Edit > Transpose = PRE or OCT per channel to taste.
Patch OUT 1–4 to four VCOs' V/Oct inputs.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — four stepped quantized arp lines, -3 to +7V, advancing on each IN clock edge.
patchIdeas.hear: Four arpeggios moving at the clock rate, each tracing its own scale + mode + spread; TRSP shifts the entire quartet up/down without breaking the scale.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* ARPG: enabled on CH 1-4
- Clock (Out) c> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Clock (Out) c> Bard Quartet (IN 2)
- Clock (Out) c> Bard Quartet (IN 3)
- Clock (Out) c> Bard Quartet (IN 4)
- LFO (CV) >> Bard Quartet (TRSP) // transposes all channels
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Shift register melody
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 carries a live melody; CH 2–4 echo it one gate-edge behind via A# Shift Register option.
steps: Press Edit (button D). On the keyboard, press the GT IN column button for channels 2, 3, 4 so GT IN is assigned to each (required for Shift Register).
Press Edit + µTune together to open Options. Select CH 2, press keyboard A# — LED confirms Shift Register ON. Repeat for CH 3 and CH 4.
Press Edit + µTune again to exit Options.
Program the same scale on CH 1–4 (or different scales for stylized harmonization).
Leave IN 2, IN 3, IN 4 UNPATCHED — 0V is required at those inputs for Shift Register to engage.
Patch your pitch-CV sequencer → IN 1 (jack 3).
Patch a clock → GT IN (jack 2) — each rising edge shifts CH 1 → CH 2 → CH 3 → CH 4.
Patch OUT 1–4 to four VCOs; mix with short envelopes to hear the cascade.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — cascading pitches, -3 to +7V; OUT n = OUT n-1 from the previous GT IN clock.
patchIdeas.hear: A single melody smeared across four voices like a tape-loop delay made of pitches: CH 1 sings the current note, CH 2 sings the previous, CH 3 the one before that, CH 4 four steps behind.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* CH 2-4: A# shift register mode
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Clock (Gate) g> Bard Quartet (Gate Input) // advances shift register
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: SATB Vocal Quartet (Bass/Tenor/Alto/Soprano)
patchIdeas.patch: Four-part chorale: each of the 8 harmonies stores a root-position 4-note chord, HRMN CV walks the song, GT IN fires Track & Hold.
steps: Plan your progression (e.g. I-IV-V-vi-ii-V-I-V) and set Harmony pot to position 1.
Press Channel button 1, program CH 1 to the bass note of chord I using keyboard buttons. Repeat for CH 2 = tenor, CH 3 = alto, CH 4 = soprano.
Turn Harmony pot to position 2 and program the 4 voices for chord IV; repeat for all 8 harmonies.
Press Edit, then on the keyboard enable GT IN for all 4 channels so GT IN acts as Track & Hold per voice.
Patch a slow CV sequencer (8 steps, ±5V) → HRMN (jack 6) to walk the progression.
Patch a clock → GT IN (jack 2) — each rising edge latches the current chord.
Patch OUT 1 → Bass VCO, OUT 2 → Tenor VCO, OUT 3 → Alto VCO, OUT 4 → Soprano VCO.
Patch TRIG OUT (jack 1) → envelope trigger so each note-change re-articulates all four voices.
Leave IN 1–4 unpatched — the programmed scales output the root-position notes directly.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — four V/Oct chorale voices, -3 to +7V; TRIG OUT pulses on note change.
patchIdeas.hear: A chord-by-chord chorale: bass walks the tonic, tenor/alto/soprano move in strict four-part voicings as HRMN advances. TRIG OUT re-bowing all voices on each chord change for articulated changes.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* Arp: OFF · 8 harmonies = 4-note chord voicings
- Sequencer (Out) >> Bard Quartet (HRMN)
- Clock (Out) g> Bard Quartet (Gate)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> Tenor VCO (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> Alto VCO (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> Soprano VCO (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (TRIG) t> Envelope (Trig) // retrigger all voices

patchIdeas.name: Harmonic Orbit (HRMN-driven progression)
patchIdeas.patch: Slow LFO sweeps 8 harmonies programmed as I-vi-IV-V-iii-IV-ii-V; lead on CH 1 rides over S&H pads on CH 2–4.
steps: Press Edit + µTune to open Options; press keyboard C (Sample & Hold) so GT IN reacts to rising edges instead of levels. Exit Options.
With Harmony pot at position 1, program CH 1 = melody scale (e.g. C major), CH 2/3/4 = chord tones for I (C-E-G).
Advance Harmony pot to position 2, re-program chord tones for vi (A-C-E); repeat for positions 3–8 covering I-vi-IV-V-iii-IV-ii-V.
Press Edit, enable GT IN on CH 2, CH 3, CH 4 only (leave CH 1 Track & Hold OFF so the lead tracks freely).
Patch a slow LFO (period = 8 bars) → HRMN (jack 6).
Patch your melody sequencer → IN 1 (jack 3) — this drives the lead.
Patch a bar-rate clock → GT IN (jack 2) — latches pad voicings once per chord change.
Patch OUT 1 → Lead VCO, OUT 2–4 → three Pad VCOs.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — continuous quantized lead, -3 to +7V; OUT 2–4 — S&H pad voicings that update on clock rising edge.
patchIdeas.hear: A flowing lead line over a drifting 8-chord cycle: the lead moves in time with the sequencer while the pad voicings shift on each clock edge, creating the classic cinematic loop.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* Harmonies 1-8: I-vi-IV-V-iii-IV-ii-V
- Slow LFO (Out) >> Bard Quartet (HRMN)
- Sequencer (Out) p> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Clock (Out) g> Bard Quartet (Gate) // S&H pads
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> Lead VCO (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> Pad VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> Pad VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> Pad VCO 3 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Shift Register Chord Crawler
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 receives a melody; A# Shift Register cascades it to CH 2–4 with octave offsets via the Edit-menu Octave column.
steps: Press Edit + µTune to enter Options; enable A# (Shift Register) on CH 2, CH 3, CH 4. Exit Options.
Press Edit (D). On the keyboard OCT column, set CH 1 = 0, CH 2 = +0 (same), CH 3 = +1, CH 4 = +2 (or any inversion offsets you want). Exit Edit.
Also in Edit, enable GT IN on CH 2, CH 3, CH 4 (required for Shift Register triggering).
Program the same scale on all four channels so the shifted notes remain in key.
Leave IN 2, IN 3, IN 4 UNPATCHED (0V required for Shift Register engagement).
Patch your pitch sequencer → IN 1 (jack 3).
Patch a clock → GT IN (jack 2) — each rising edge walks the shift register CH 1→2→3→4.
Patch OUT 1–4 to a 4-in mixer, sum to one audio bus for rolling voicing texture.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Mixer sum of OUT 1–4 — a four-note rolling voicing that refreshes on each GT IN pulse.
patchIdeas.hear: A self-harmonizing melody: each note you play gets picked up by the next channel one clock later at a different octave, stacking into a four-note moving voicing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* CH 2-4: A# shift register · octaves +0/+5/+7/+12
- Sequencer (Out) p> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Clock (Out) g> Bard Quartet (Gate) // rising edge shifts
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) -> Mixer (In 2)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) -> Mixer (In 3)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) -> Mixer (In 4)

patchIdeas.name: Polytonal Quartet (4 keys at once)
patchIdeas.patch: Each channel locked to a different key center for deliberate polytonal drift — no TRSP, no grouping, no channel interaction.
steps: Press Edit + µTune to enter Options; confirm F# (No Interaction) is ON for CH 2, CH 3, CH 4 so channels stay fully independent. Exit Options.
Press Channel 1 and program a C major scale on the keyboard.
Press Channel 2 and program F# major (the tritone against C).
Press Channel 3 and program Bb Dorian.
Press Channel 4 and program E minor pentatonic.
Patch four free-running LFOs (different rates) → IN 1, IN 2, IN 3, IN 4.
Leave TRSP unpatched so the polytonal relationships never collapse.
Patch OUT 1–4 to four VCOs and send each through its own filter/VCA chain.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — four independent quantized CVs each locked to its own key, -3 to +7V.
patchIdeas.hear: Four voices that never share a tonal center: brief consonances emerge when unrelated LFOs cross, but the overall effect is shimmering polytonal ambiguity.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* CH1=C maj · CH2=F# maj · CH3=Bb Dor · CH4=E min pent
- LFO 1 (Out) >> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- LFO 2 (Out) >> Bard Quartet (IN 2)
- LFO 3 (Out) >> Bard Quartet (IN 3)
- LFO 4 (Out) >> Bard Quartet (IN 4)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Parallel Harmonizer (Root/3rd/5th/Octave)
patchIdeas.patch: Melody CV multed to all four channels; same scale but with degree offsets baked into each channel's programmed notes.
steps: On CH 1, program your home scale (e.g. C major).
On CH 2, program the same scale but with each key shifted up a 3rd (D→F, E→G, etc.) so CH 2 quantizes to a third above.
On CH 3, program the scale shifted up a 5th; on CH 4, program it shifted up an octave.
Press Edit + µTune; confirm F# (No Interaction) ON for CH 2–4 so multed CVs quantize independently per channel.
Patch your melody VCO's CV → a 1-to-4 mult.
Patch mult outs → IN 1, IN 2, IN 3, IN 4.
Turn the Harmony pot slowly while playing to morph between I/IV/V chord-quality banks across all 8 harmonies you programmed.
Patch OUT 1–4 → four VCOs, mix to one channel.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — root / 3rd / 5th / octave of the same melody, each -3 to +7V.
patchIdeas.hear: A four-voice parallel harmony following the melody line in lock-step; turning HRMN re-colors all four voices to whatever chord-bank you programmed into that harmony slot.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* Same scale all CH · offsets +0/+3rd/+5th/+oct
- Melody VCO (CV) -> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out1) p> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Mult (Out2) p> Bard Quartet (IN 2)
- Mult (Out3) p> Bard Quartet (IN 3)
- Mult (Out4) p> Bard Quartet (IN 4)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Arp + 3 Drones
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1–3 hold root/3rd/5th per harmony, CH 4 arpeggiates the same chord; HRMN sequencer walks progression; TRIG OUT doubles as drum accent.
steps: Program CH 1 = root, CH 2 = 3rd, CH 3 = 5th for all 8 harmonies, forming triads.
Press ARPG, hold Channel 4 + ARPG to turn CH 4 into an arpeggiator (LED blinks).
In the Arp menu, set CH 4 Mode = Up (▲) and Octave Spread = 2; press ARPG again to exit.
Press Edit; assign TRIG OUT to CH 4 (or CH 1 — keyboard T OUT column) so drums trigger from note-changes on that channel.
Leave IN 1, IN 2, IN 3 unpatched — the scale's root output sits on OUT 1–3.
Patch a clock → IN 4 (jack 3) — advances the arp.
Patch a slow sequencer → HRMN (jack 6) to walk chord changes.
Patch OUT 1–3 → three drone VCOs; OUT 4 → arp VCO.
Patch TRIG OUT (jack 1) → drum accent input.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–3 — three sustained chord-tone CVs; OUT 4 — arp CV; TRIG OUT — accent pulses on arp note changes.
patchIdeas.hear: Three drones sustain the current triad while CH 4 arpeggiates that triad across two octaves. Every arp step fires a drum accent, tying percussion to melody.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* CH4: Arp UP · octave spread 2
- Clock (Out) c> Bard Quartet (IN 4) // arp clock
- Sequencer (Out) >> Bard Quartet (HRMN)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> Drone VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> Drone VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> Drone VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> Arp VCO (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (TRIG) t> Drum (Accent)

patchIdeas.name: Quad Arp Polyrhythm
patchIdeas.patch: All four channels run as arpeggiators with spreads 1/2/3/4; clock divider feeds IN 1–4; HRMN walks chord banks. (Expander optional — clocks route directly to IN jacks.)
steps: Press ARPG, hold Channel 1 + ARPG to arm CH 1 as arp; repeat for CH 2, CH 3, CH 4.
In the Arp menu, set CH 1 spread = 1, CH 2 = 2, CH 3 = 3, CH 4 = 4 (use keyboard OCT column). Mode = Up for all. Exit ARPG.
Program a shared triad scale on each channel for all 8 harmonies.
Patch a master clock → clock divider; route /3 → IN 1, /5 → IN 2, /7 → IN 3, /8 → IN 4.
Patch a slow LFO → HRMN so chords drift under the polyrhythm.
Patch OUT 1–4 → four VCOs, each with its own envelope on TRIG OUT assigned per channel (or individual envelopes triggered from the same clock divider outs).
Mix the four voices to hear the polyrhythmic interlock.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — four arp lines advancing at /3, /5, /7, /8 of the master clock respectively.
patchIdeas.hear: A polyrhythmic chord cloud: short arpeggios (spread 1) chatter against long ones (spread 4); every 840 master ticks the whole pattern realigns.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* All CH: Arp · spreads 1/2/3/4 · Expander required
- Clock Divider (/3) c> Expander (Gate 1)
- Clock Divider (/5) c> Expander (Gate 2)
- Clock Divider (/7) c> Expander (Gate 3)
- Clock Divider (/8) c> Expander (Gate 4)
- LFO (Out) >> Bard Quartet (HRMN)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Microtonal Raga Channel
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 retuned to a raga via µTune ±50¢; CH 2–4 stay 12-TET Ionian pad; CH 1 TRSP = POST so raga intervals survive transposition.
steps: On CH 1, program the raga's chromatic notes across the keyboard (e.g. komal re, shuddha ga, tivra ma).
Press µTune (button E). Press Channel 1, then press each keyboard note that needs detuning and rotate Harmony pot — CW = up to +50¢, CCW = down to -50¢. Blinking key confirms detune.
Exit µTune. Program CH 2, CH 3, CH 4 = plain C Ionian (no microtuning).
Press Edit. On keyboard, set CH 1 TRSP column = POST (post-quantize transpose preserves the tuned intervals).
Set CH 2/3/4 TRSP = PRE or OFF so Ionian pads transpose the standard way.
Patch a drone CV → IN 1 (jack 3).
Patch a keyboard controller CV → TRSP (jack 4) for live transposition.
Patch a clock → GT IN (jack 2); enable GT IN on CH 2–4 in Edit so pads latch.
Patch OUT 1 → Sitar VCO, OUT 2–4 → three pad VCOs.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — microtonal sitar CV with raga intervals preserved; OUT 2–4 — 12-TET pad CVs.
patchIdeas.hear: A sitar-like lead that moves in authentic raga intervals over a Western Ionian pad. POST-transpose on CH 1 keeps the microtuned intervals intact; PRE on the pads lets them modulate normally.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* CH1: raga scale + µTune · TRSP=POST · CH2-4=Ionian
- Drone VCO (CV) p> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Keyboard (CV) >> Bard Quartet (TRSP)
- Clock (Out) g> Bard Quartet (Gate)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> Sitar VCO (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> Pad VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> Pad VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> Pad VCO 3 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: TRIG-OUT Percussion Conductor
patchIdeas.patch: Random S&H feeds CH 1; TRIG OUT fires drums on every detected note-change; GT IN latches melody via a deliberate sequencer gate.
steps: Program CH 1 with a sparse scale (pentatonic keeps rhythmic interest obvious).
Press Edit; on the keyboard T OUT column, assign TRIG OUT to CH 1 only so only CH 1 note-changes fire triggers.
Enable GT IN on CH 1 in Edit so the sequencer gate gates the Track & Hold.
Patch a random S&H (or noisy CV) → IN 1 (jack 3).
Patch your melody sequencer's gate → GT IN (jack 2) — dictates WHEN CH 1 latches new pitches.
Patch OUT 1 → Lead VCO V/Oct.
Patch TRIG OUT (jack 1) → drum envelope trigger to fire a drum on every note-change.
Also mult TRIG OUT → a rhythm sequencer's clock to vary percussion on pitch changes.
Tune the S&H rate against the sequencer gate rate to balance how often new notes (and thus drum hits) occur.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — quantized pentatonic melody; TRIG OUT — pulse on every CH 1 note-change, 0–5V.
patchIdeas.hear: A chaotic random source becomes a melodic lead whose every note-change cues a drum. Drum density is coupled to harmonic density, tying rhythm directly to pitch motion.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
- S&H (Out) >> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Bard Quartet (Gate)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> Lead VCO (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (TRIG) t> Drum Envelope (Trig) // fires on note change
- Bard Quartet (TRIG) c> Rhythm Sequencer (Clock)

patchIdeas.name: Split TRSP Routing (Pre/Post/Octave)
patchIdeas.patch: One keyboard CV into TRSP produces four simultaneous results — CH 1 = PRE (chromatic), CH 2 = POST (diatonic), CH 3 = OCTAVE, CH 4 = OFF.
steps: Program the same diatonic scale on CH 1, CH 2, CH 3. Leave CH 4 as a static drone scale (or whichever static tone you want).
Press Edit. On keyboard, TRSP column:
• CH 1 → press PRE (transposes before quantization — chromatic shift, notes jump in and out of scale).
• CH 2 → press POST (transposes after quantization — diatonic shift, stays in key).
• CH 3 → press OCT (transposes in whole octaves only).
• CH 4 → press nothing (TRSP disabled — stays put).
Patch a 1V/oct keyboard CV → TRSP (jack 4).
Patch a clock → GT IN (jack 2); enable GT IN on CH 1–4 in Edit for latched pitches.
Patch a pitch sequencer → IN 1 (jack 3) to drive the shared melody.
Patch OUT 1–4 → four VCOs; mix and compare the four transpose behaviors side-by-side.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — chromatic-shifted melody; OUT 2 — diatonic-shifted melody; OUT 3 — octave-shifted melody; OUT 4 — static drone.
patchIdeas.hear: Four simultaneous answers to the question 'what does transpose mean?': chromatic jumps (CH 1) vs diatonic walk (CH 2) vs pure octave (CH 3) vs fixed drone (CH 4) — a perfect ear-training patch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* CH1 TRSP=PRE · CH2=POST · CH3=OCT · CH4=OFF
- Keyboard (CV) >> Bard Quartet (TRSP)
- Clock (Out) g> Bard Quartet (Gate)
- Sequencer (Out) p> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Grouped Wide Chord + Independent Bass/Lead
patchIdeas.patch: CH 2 + CH 3 grouped for wide pad sharing scale (with CH 3 +1 oct); CH 1 independent bass; CH 4 independent lead; slow sequencer walks HRMN.
steps: Press Channel 2 (hold) and press Channel 3 — LEDs light together, confirming CH 2 + CH 3 are grouped (scale is shared).
With the group active, program a chord-tone scale (CH 2/CH 3 now both use this scale).
Press Edit, set CH 3 OCT = +1 (keyboard OCT column) so CH 3 plays an octave above CH 2.
Press Channel 1, program independent bass scale. Press Channel 4, program independent lead scale.
Patch bass sequencer → IN 1, lead melody sequencer → IN 4.
Leave IN 2, IN 3 unpatched so the grouped pad plays the programmed root tone.
Patch a slow sequencer → HRMN to walk chord changes.
Patch a clock → GT IN (jack 2); in Edit, enable GT IN on CH 2 and CH 3 for latched pad.
Patch OUT 1 → Bass VCO, OUT 2 → Pad A, OUT 3 → Pad B (+oct), OUT 4 → Lead VCO.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — bass line; OUT 2/3 — wide grouped pad (root + octave); OUT 4 — lead line; all -3 to +7V.
patchIdeas.hear: Full-band texture from one module: a moving bassline underneath a wide two-octave pad, with an independent lead floating over the top as HRMN shifts the chord under everything.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* CH2+CH3 grouped (+oct) · CH1=bass · CH4=lead
- Bass Seq (Out) p> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Pad Trig (Gate) g> Bard Quartet (Gate)
- Melody Seq (Out) p> Bard Quartet (IN 4)
- Slow Seq (Out) >> Bard Quartet (HRMN)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> Pad VCO A (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> Pad VCO B (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> Lead VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Generative Feedback Patch
patchIdeas.patch: Attenuated OUT 1 → IN 2 → OUT 2 → IN 3 → OUT 3 → IN 4: a quantized feedback chain across 4 different scales; TRIG OUT cascades S&H.
steps: Program CH 1 = pentatonic, CH 2 = whole-tone, CH 3 = blues, CH 4 = dorian.
Press Edit + µTune and confirm F# (No Interaction) ON for CH 2–4 so feedback signals quantize without shift-register behavior. Exit Options.
Patch a slow random LFO → IN 1 (jack 3) as the seed.
Patch OUT 1 → external attenuator (~0.3 gain) to keep voltages in range, attenuator → IN 2.
Patch OUT 2 → IN 3 directly; OUT 3 → IN 4 directly.
Patch TRIG OUT (jack 1) → a clock divider; divider output → GT IN (jack 2) to latch all voices periodically.
In Edit, enable GT IN on all four channels so the divider clock re-samples the chain.
Patch OUT 1–4 → four VCOs to hear the cascading scale-hops.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — a quantized feedback chain; each OUT's pitch depends on the previous OUT's value at the last GT IN pulse.
patchIdeas.hear: A generative pitch web that never quite repeats: each note in each scale conditions the next in a different scale, producing melodic constellations that drift but stay musical.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* CH1=Pent · CH2=Whole · CH3=Blues · CH4=Dorian
- Random LFO (Out) >> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) -> Attenuator (In) // ~0.3 gain
- Attenuator (Out) >> Bard Quartet (IN 2)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) >> Bard Quartet (IN 3)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) >> Bard Quartet (IN 4)
- Bard Quartet (TRIG) c> Clock Divider (In)
- Clock Divider (Out) g> Bard Quartet (Gate)

patchIdeas.name: Chord Quality Morph via Microtune
patchIdeas.patch: Static CVs on CH 1–4 play root/3rd/5th/7th; 3rd-degree note detuned from 0¢ (major) toward -50¢ (minor) across harmonies; HRMN LFO morphs chord quality.
steps: On Harmony 1, program CH 1 = root (e.g. C), CH 2 = major 3rd (E), CH 3 = 5th (G), CH 4 = 7th (B).
Copy the same notes into all 8 harmonies (ARPG copy/paste — hold ARPG, pick source harmony, release on target; see manual §Intra-slot manipulation).
Press µTune, press Channel 2 (3rd voice), press keyboard E (the 3rd).
Turn Harmony pot to position 1, µTune pot to 0¢. Advance to position 2, set -10¢. Continue stepping: H3 -20¢, H4 -30¢, H5 -40¢, H6 -50¢ (minor), H7 -40¢, H8 -20¢ — arc back toward major.
Exit µTune.
Patch four fixed-voltage sources (offset module or static 0V / +0.33V / +0.58V / +0.92V) → IN 1, IN 2, IN 3, IN 4 to fix root/3/5/7.
Patch a slow LFO → HRMN (jack 6) — this morphs the 3rd's tuning over time.
Patch OUT 1–4 → four VCOs; mix to one bus.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 = root; OUT 2 = morphing 3rd (0¢↔-50¢); OUT 3 = 5th; OUT 4 = 7th.
patchIdeas.hear: A static chord that slowly morphs quality: major → neutral → minor → neutral → major as HRMN sweeps H1→H8. Only the 3rd bends; root/5th/7th stay fixed. Continuous chord-coloring without any pitch sequencing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* 3rd detune 0¢→-17¢→-50¢ across H1-8
- Static CV 1 (Out) p> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Static CV 2 (Out) p> Bard Quartet (IN 2)
- Static CV 3 (Out) p> Bard Quartet (IN 3)
- Static CV 4 (Out) p> Bard Quartet (IN 4)
- Slow LFO (Out) >> Bard Quartet (HRMN) // morphs maj↔min
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Scene Recall Song Mode
patchIdeas.patch: 10 memory slots = 10 song sections, loaded manually via Memory menu (keyboard buttons 1–10); HRMN walks chords within each scene. Memory slots are NOT CV-addressable — recall is keyboard-only.
steps: Program Slot 1 = intro (CH 1–4 scales + 8 harmonies). Press Edit + ARPG to open Memory menu (Edit LED steady, ARPG blinks).
Press D# (Save), then press keyboard slot button 1 to confirm save. Edit + ARPG to exit.
Modify scales for verse section, then save to slot 2 via the same procedure. Repeat for 10 slots = 10 song sections.
To perform: press Edit + ARPG to enter Memory, press C# (Load), then press the slot button (1–10) you want to recall — Bard Quartet loads that scene's full state.
Patch a pitch sequencer → IN 1 (jack 3).
Patch a sequencer → HRMN (jack 6) to walk chord changes within the loaded scene.
Patch a clock → GT IN (jack 2); in Edit, assign GT IN per channel as needed.
Patch OUT 1–4 → four VCOs. Recall scenes manually (or via a MIDI foot controller sending keyboard button presses) to advance the song.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — four quantized voices whose full programming (scale + harmonies + arp + µTune) changes on each manual Load operation.
patchIdeas.hear: A complete song arrangement in one module: intro, verse, chorus, bridge, etc. each stored as a scene; within each scene HRMN walks the chord progression. Scene changes produce full re-voicings, not just chord substitutions.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* 10 memory slots = song sections (manual load via Edit+ARPG)
- Sequencer (Out) >> Bard Quartet (HRMN)
- Pitch Seq (Out) p> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- Clock (Out) g> Bard Quartet (Gate)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Quad S&H Chord Cloud
patchIdeas.patch: Four incommensurate LFOs (0.10/0.13/0.17/0.23 Hz) → IN 1–4; slow clock gates S&H; pentatonic on all channels; octaves -2/-1/0/+1; TRIG OUT taps tape delay.
steps: Press Edit + µTune to enter Options; press keyboard C to switch GT IN behavior to Sample & Hold (rising-edge). Exit Options.
Program the same pentatonic scale on all four channels (press each Channel button and set keyboard notes to pentatonic degrees).
Press Edit; on keyboard OCT column set CH 1 = -2, CH 2 = -1, CH 3 = 0, CH 4 = +1. Also enable GT IN on all four channels. Exit Edit.
Patch four slow free-running LFOs with incommensurate rates (0.10, 0.13, 0.17, 0.23 Hz) → IN 1, IN 2, IN 3, IN 4.
Patch a slow clock (e.g. 1 Hz) → GT IN (jack 2) — every rising edge S&Hs all four channels simultaneously.
Press Edit; on keyboard T OUT column, assign TRIG OUT to the most active channel (CH 4).
Patch OUT 1–4 → four VCOs spread across a wide octave range; mix.
Patch TRIG OUT (jack 1) → tape-delay tap input to re-time the delay on each new note.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — four pentatonic pitches that refresh synchronously on each GT IN rising edge, spread across -2 to +1 octaves; TRIG OUT taps tape delay.
patchIdeas.hear: A shimmering pentatonic cloud: all four voices change together on each clock pulse, but because the LFOs are at unrelated rates, each voice picks a different random pentatonic pitch every time. The tape delay stretches and smears each new cloud into the next.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BARD QUARTET:
* Pentatonic all CH · oct -2/-1/0/+1
- LFO 1 (0.10Hz) >> Bard Quartet (IN 1)
- LFO 2 (0.13Hz) >> Bard Quartet (IN 2)
- LFO 3 (0.17Hz) >> Bard Quartet (IN 3)
- LFO 4 (0.23Hz) >> Bard Quartet (IN 4)
- Slow Clock (Out) g> Bard Quartet (Gate)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (OUT 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)
- Bard Quartet (TRIG) t> Tape Delay (Tap)
